_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to expand our vocabulary by exploring synonyms (words with similar meanings) and antonyms (words with opposite meanings), and by using words from our reading.

_source.w: We are learning to expand our vocabulary by exploring synonyms (words with similar meanings) and antonyms (words with opposite meanings), and by using words from our reading.
y: A rich vocabulary gives us more choices for expressing exactly what we mean.
Knowing synonyms helps us avoid repeating the same words in our writing.
Understanding antonyms helps us grasp the full range of meanings in language.
eg: We brainstorm synonyms for common words like 'happy' — 'joyful', 'cheerful', 'delighted', 'thrilled'.
We find antonym pairs in our reading — 'ancient/modern', 'brave/cowardly', 'expand/shrink'.
We replace overused words in our writing with more precise synonyms.
We play a synonym and antonym matching game to build our word knowledge.
We use a thesaurus to find alternatives for common words in our writing.
We keep a vocabulary journal where we collect interesting new words from our reading.
